MORAL.Definition: related to ideas of right and wrong behaviour

MODEST.Definition: not very large, powerful, or impressive

VISIBILITY.Definition: how far they could see clearly

VALLEYS.Definition: low land between hills or mountains

CRUDE.Definition: very simple and not well made

ANAESTHETIC.Definition: a drug that stops you feeling pain during treatment

PRESSING.Definition: very urgent and needing attention quickly

COUNTERINTUITIVELY.Definition: in a way that is the opposite of what people would expect

HYDRATED.Definition: having enough water in the body

FASHIONED.Definition: made it in a simple way

RAYS.Definition: thin lines of light from the sun

MELT.Definition: change it from solid into liquid

OVERHEAD.Definition: above your head or in the sky above them

COLLAPSED.Definition: suddenly fell down or lost strength

SEARCH PARTY.Definition: a group of people looking for them

COLLAPSE.Definition: a sudden loss of strength or hope

CLARITY.Definition: a clear understanding of the situation

TIN.Definition: a small metal container for food

REITERATE.Definition: say it again to make it clear

COMA.Definition: a long, deep state of unconsciousness

CAME ROUND.Definition: woke up or became conscious again

SUMMIT.Definition: reach the very top of it

INSTINCT.Definition: a natural feeling that told him what to do

AGAINST ALL ODDS.Definition: even though it had seemed very unlikely

IMPROVISED.Definition: made quickly using what was available

SLED.Definition: a vehicle used for sliding over snow
